title: Neomammillaria perbella
taxonomy: Neomammillaria perbella is now classified as Mammillaria perbella. It belongs to the family Cactaceae, commonly known as cacti. The species is further grouped into the subfamily Cactoideae. Within this subfamily, Mammillaria perbella is placed in the tribe Cacteae. This classification reflects its characteristics as a type of flowering cactus plant.

intro: Neomammillaria perbella, also known as the Desert Cactus, is a species of succulent cactus native to Mexico, characterized by its vibrant pink to red flowers and robust, compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of fever, wounds, and digestive issues. Neomammillaria perbella contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neomammillaria perbella include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
